---
description: andy setup — inspect the repository and install approved agent skills
argument-hint: "[search hints]"
---

You are running the andy `setup` workflow.

Input: `$ARGUMENTS`

Steps:

1. Resolve the andy harness root. Use the first directory that contains both `AGENTS.md` and `core/workflows/`, in this order: `$ANDY_HARNESS_ROOT` (if set), `${CODEX_HOME:-$HOME/.codex}/active-harness`, `${CODEX_HOME:-$HOME/.codex}/harnesses/andy`, `./.andy/harness`. If none exists, tell the user to run the andy installer.
2. Read `<harness-root>/core/workflows/command-router.md` and `<harness-root>/core/workflows/setup.md`, then follow them.
3. Treat the current working directory as the target development repository. Inspect repository instructions, manifests, tooling, and docs narrowly; do not read secrets or `.env` files.
4. Use the input as optional high-priority skill search hints. Search for relevant agent skills with GitHub CLI `gh skill` commands when available.
5. Before installing anything, present recommendations and ask the user to approve all, some, or none. After approval, prefer project-local agent-neutral installation under `.agents/skills/` or the closest universal/project-scope GitHub CLI target.
6. Verify installed skills and report evidence.

If the input is empty, infer skill search queries from the current repository inventory.
